bonuses.leadin: This artist's numerous sculptures of hands were often dramatic and misshapen. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This artist's numerous sculptures of hands were often dramatic and misshapen. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this sculptor who exaggerated the hands of some doomed noblemen in The Burghers of Calais ("cah-LAY").

Two intertwined<u> right</u> hands touch in a Rodin sc<u>ulptur</u>e named for one of these buildings. Statues are suspended from steel cables in one of these buildings designed by Oscar Niemeyer that has sixteen hyperboloid columns.
This American sculptor, who worked in Rome, casted the life-sized clasped hands of the Brownings for a bronze sculpture. Her other works include Zenobia in Chains.
parts_sanitized: Name this sculptor who exaggerated the hands of some doomed noblemen in The Burghers of Calais ("cah-LAY").

Two intertwined right hands touch in a Rodin sculpture named for one of these buildings. Statues are suspended from steel cables in one of these buildings designed by Oscar Niemeyer that has sixteen hyperboloid columns.
This American sculptor, who worked in Rome, casted the life-sized clasped hands of the Brownings for a bronze sculpture. Her other works include Zenobia in Chains.
answers: cathedrals [or The Cathedral; or La Cathédrale; or the Cathedral of Brasília; or Catedral Metropolitana de Brasília]
answers_sanitized: cathedrals [or The Cathedral; or La Cathedrale; or the Cathedral of Brasilia; or Catedral Metropolitana de Brasilia]

bonuses.leadin: This city is nicknamed “Fantasy Island” because of its modernist architecture and utopian layout, and much of its urban planning was executed by Lucio Costa. For 10 points each:
parts: Oscar Niemeyer designed many buildings for what South American capital, a planned city finished in 1960?

Sixteen hyperboloid columns characterize the exterior of this type of building in Brasilia, which was designed by Niemeyer. One of these buildings with a glass exterior was designed by Philip Johnson in Garden Grove, California.

The centerpiece of Niemeyer’s Pampulha Project was a building of this type. Dale Chihuly’s Fiori di Como is located in another building of this type, while one in Montreal incorporates the French and Quebec Pavilions from Expo ‘67.
answers: cathedrals [accept Cathedral of Brasilia; accept Crystal Cathedral; prompt on “churches”]
answers_sanitized: cathedrals [accept Cathedral of Brasilia; accept Crystal Cathedral; prompt on "churches"]

Sixteen hyperboloid columns characterize the exterior of this type of building in Brasilia, which was designed by Niemeyer. One of these buildings with a glass exterior was designed by Philip Johnson in Garden Grove, California.

The centerpiece of Niemeyer's Pampulha Project was a building of this type. Dale Chihuly's Fiori di Como is located in another building of this type, while one in Montreal incorporates the French and Quebec Pavilions from Expo '67.
